title: Everyone Is Special
lesson: Everyone looks different and likes different things, and those differences make us special friends.
prompts: In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits cross-legged on the far left of a small circle, chatting and gesturing with one hand, with a friendly smile. To the right sit {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}, chatting together. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left, leaning forward with hands on their knees, with a curious, interested expression, watching a friend share. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} holds up a favourite picture book while {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} leans in to look. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left proudly holding up their own favourite toy, with a happy, sharing smile. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} clap and look interested. Exactly three children in the frame and no one else. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — four friends, and only four children in total, no extra or duplicate child, exactly four not five, stand in a single row noticing how different they all look. From left to right: first the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image on the far left, laughing and gesturing playfully, with a warm smile; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, who is the only child wearing red dungarees; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN}; then {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}. Each is one single distinct child, each looking different, laughing and waving. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left holding hands in a circle with friends, with a joyful smile. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} and {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} hold hands too, all together. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, golden light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left in a happy group hug with friends, with a big warm smile. To the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} hug in close, all delighted. Exactly four children in the frame and no one else. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: In a bright classroom on a colourful alphabet rug, the child (far left) sits cross-legged in a small circle, chatting and gesturing, while three other children sit at right chatting together.
On the classroom rug, the child (far left) leans forward with hands on knees, curious, watching a friend share; at right a girl holds up a favourite picture book while a boy leans in to look.
On the classroom rug, the child (far left) proudly holds up their own favourite toy with a sharing smile while two friends at right clap, interested — exactly three children.
On the classroom rug, four children stand in a single row noticing how different they all look — child far left laughing and gesturing, then the three friends — exactly four, no duplicates.
On the classroom rug, the child (far left) holds hands in a circle with three friends at right, all joyful — different and special, side by side.
On the classroom rug in golden light, the child (far left) is in a happy group hug with three friends at right, all delighted — exactly four children. Everyone is special.
texts: You sat in a circle with your friends on the colourful classroom rug. Everyone was chatting happily.
One friend held up her favourite picture book to share. You watched, curious and interested.
Then you showed your favourite toy. "This is mine!" you said proudly. Your friends clapped.
You all looked so different from each other — and that made you laugh and smile together.
You held hands in one big circle, all of you together. Different and special, side by side.
Then came a great big group hug! Everyone is special, and that makes the best friends.
